Tests that the "prototype" property does not exist in the Number object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that iterates over the properties of the `Number` object in JavaScript. It checks if any of the properties is named `prototype`. If the `prototype` property is found, the script calls the `assert` function with `false`, which typically indicates a failure condition in testing or debugging contexts. This script provides narrow functionality, specifically for verifying the absence of the `prototype` property in the `Number` object during iteration.
